▲ Page top
I don't get it
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2002 at 5:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252237188
15 posts
This item looks very cool but I was disappointed to see that it had no melee stat. There are weapons that give better wis, int and mana but also give some meleee capabilities. Such as a granite face grinder it has wis10, int10, and str10 but also hits for 29 and has a delay of 36. seems to me that a real weapon is better than this eye candy.
▲ Page top
RE: I don't get it
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2002 at 4:22 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
You completely miss the point. First, Shaman is the only wis caster that can use the face grinder. Second, its a 2 handed weapon, which means you lose whatever you have in secondary. My main is a lvl 46 cleric, and if I were to stand up and start meleeing, rather than medding up, either my puller is moving too slow, or I'm playing around with some green stuff.

My gear is nothing uber, but my secondary slot item (Testament of Vanear) has as much wis, and 10 mana , as the face grinder. If I threw the cone in primary, I'd have 15 wis and 60 mana out of my melee slots. with a face grinder, I'd have 10 wis, and no mana. Oh, yeah I'd have 10 strength, which means nothing, since I can't do enough melee damage to matter anyway, even with a really expensive weapon, and the face grinder weighs 13 stone, so my carrying capacity would go DOWN by 3.My current primary item is the paw of oppala, but I would take the cone in a heartbeat, since 50 mana is a lot better than another 4 wis.

What I don't get is why you would think having a weapon would possibly be as good as this, unless of course you don't play a caster.

I am looking at it from a WIS caster perspective, but pure int casters would be in the same position, except they would probably have a stein of Maggok (or better) in secondary. Either way, not many weapons can add as much wis/int and mana to a caster's pool as those items can. Once I get my epic, I'll carry a weapon again, but until then, I'll use my paw, or upograde to this.
▲ Page top
RE: I don't get it
QuoteReply
# Mar 27 2002 at 7:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253828658
2 posts
Sure, for melees a real weapon is better than this. Anyone who can equip (and afford) a Granite Face Grinder should equip that over this. For druid and int casters, however, this is a great primary slot item. Especially for druids, who are so boring looking all around, a little eye candy goes a long way. Never underestimate form over function. =)

Lothian Grovekeeper
Druid of Tunare
<Nine Realms>
Lanys T'vyl

QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2001 at 7:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757322615
3 posts
According to this webpage, this is a lvl 55 item. Any caster which hasn't got their wiz/int over 200 by then is not playing seriously.

The INT/WIS may be nice for trade skills, but the only attractive thing I can see on this cone is the AC and the MANA.

I think it would still be a tough choice between this and a Staff of the Wheel.

Belfridge
Wizard 84th Semi-circle

RE: worthless
QuoteReply
# Jan 10 2002 at 9:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769934300
32 posts
What it means by the lvl thing, is thats the minimal lvl you should be to kill the guy that drops it, i have this nice item on my necro and he is lvl 23, and his int isnt over 200 but his resists are out the butt good, so this is a good twinking item for lower lvl chars.

Price info
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 10:05 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592758214689
7 posts
Just purchased this for 1,100 (asking price was 1,300). It's a main weapon slot item, though you can't attack with it. To the previous poster, yes, you could hit "A" and a non-agro mob would keep walking by because you're not attacking when you have the cone.
I'm not sure about the comparison to the shield because this is a different slot item, unless I'm missing something. Is the webshield also a main weapon item?
